title: Introduce End-to-End Current Probe Support and Simulation Models
description: Adds simulation-level current measurement schemas, following the existing transient voltage graph pattern where applicable.
body:
Adds simulation-level current measurement schemas, following the existing transient voltage graph pattern where applicable.

This PR includes:

- New `simulation_current_probe` schema for simulation current measurement
- New `simulation_transient_current_graph` schema mirroring `simulation_transient_voltage_graph`
- Integration into `any_circuit_element` and simulation exports
- Tests for current probe parsing, validation, union coverage, and transient current graph parsing

This is intentionally scoped to simulation only. Source/schematic current measurement should be modeled separately if needed, likely as an ammeter/current meter component with terminals rather than a voltage-probe-style schematic marker.

description: Adds first-class ammeter  prop support for current measurement components, including validated connection pairs and display configuration.
body:
Adds first-class `<ammeter />` prop support for current measurement components, including validated connection pairs and display configuration.

This PR includes:

* New `AmmeterProps` interface and `ammeterProps` schema
* Support for `pos`/`neg` and `pin1`/`pin2` connection pairs
* Validation that rejects missing, incomplete, or invalid ammeter connections
* Optional color and display configuration for current visualization
* Public exports for ammeter props and pins
* Generated props documentation and README updates
* Test coverage for valid and invalid ammeter configurations

This establishes the props-layer foundation for ammeter/current probe components and downstream current measurement workflows.

title: Adds comprehensive support for Rotated-Pill Pad Geometry across DRC, Connectivity, and Trace Validation
description: Adds comprehensive geometric support for rotated_pill SMT pads throughout the PCB validation pipeline, replacing bounding-box approximations with accurate shape-aware calculations.
body: Adds comprehensive geometric support for `rotated_pill` SMT pads throughout the PCB validation pipeline, replacing bounding-box approximations with accurate shape-aware calculations. This PR includes: * Rotated-pill pad containment checks for automatic port-to-trace association * Accurate trace-to-pill clearance calculations * Shape-aware pad-to-pad clearance analysis * Rotated-pill support in trace overlap detection * Specialized spatial bounds generation for pill geometries * Shared pill centerline and clearance utilities * Connectivity validation support for rotated-pill pad endpoints * DRC improvements that eliminate bounding-box false positives * Regression coverage for clearance, overlap, connectivity, and pad containment scenarios This significantly improves routing and DRC accuracy for modern footprint libraries by ensuring rotated-pill pads are treated as true geometric shapes rather than rectangular approximations.

title: Fix BGA detection and BGA topology generation for unevenly sized obstacle pads and super small grids.
description: What does this fix The BGA Solver now has a full pipeline that enables better handling of obstacle overlap than before, including large nodes, thanks to the merge step. This could have been a separate fix, but the issue was only discovered when rewriting from scratch: component Topology Generator was generating replacement obstacles that were single-layer only instead of multi-layer, which was bad because rectDiff was expanding into the below layers and the merging was also causing gaps. The BGA Solver now always uses the full set of available layers. Previously it was trying to restrict itself to topinner layers, which was unnecessary, so that has been removed. Much better readability overall. The MergeSolver still needs a rewrite as the logic isnt fully clear yet, but that is planned for later. Changed what is detected as BGA: the current logic requires at least a 33 matrix to work properly, so the detection logic was updated to reflect this constraint. Changed the SOIC detection logic to be independent of the BGA detection logic (required for tests to pass).
body:
## What does this fix

- The BGA Solver now has a full pipeline that enables better handling of obstacle overlap than before, including large nodes, thanks to the merge step.

- This could have been a separate fix, but the issue was only discovered when rewriting from scratch: `component Topology Generator` was generating replacement obstacles that were single-layer only instead of multi-layer, which was bad because `rectDiff` was expanding into the below layers and the merging was also causing gaps.

- The BGA Solver now always uses the full set of available layers. Previously it was trying to restrict itself to top/inner layers, which was unnecessary, so that has been removed.

- Much better readability overall. The `MergeSolver` still needs a rewrite as the logic isn't fully clear yet, but that is planned for later.

- Changed what is detected as BGA: the current logic requires at least a 3×3 matrix to work properly, so the detection logic was updated to reflect this constraint.

- Changed the SOIC detection logic to be independent of the BGA detection logic (required for tests to pass).

## AI Usage

This PR uses Codex. The visualization code is entirely AI-generated, the Merge Solver is entirely AI-generated, and the rest is AI-assisted but mostly hand-written.

title: Fix PackSolver2 initial rotation handling
description: Fixes PackSolver2 to honor availableRotationDegrees for packed components and preserves ccwRotationOffset for static components, along with adding regression tests for rotation handling.
body:
- Make `PackSolver2` honor `availableRotationDegrees[0]` when it directly creates packed components
- Preserve explicit `ccwRotationOffset` precedence for static components
- Add regression tests for first dynamic component rotation and static component rotation

**Why**
`SingleComponentPackSolver` already respected `availableRotationDegrees`, but `PackSolver2` had direct construction paths that defaulted rotation to `0`. That could make callers pass a single allowed rotation while the packed output still reported `0`.

title: fix(core): populate pcb_via.layers with all spanned layers
description: Fixes incorrect layer reporting for vias on multi-layer boards by populating pcb_via.layers with all layers spanned between fromLayer and toLayer, ensuring accurate copper pour interactions and DRC checks.
body:
## Summary

- `pcb_via.layers` listed only `[fromLayer, toLayer]` (the `// TODO calculate layers inbetween top and bottom using layer count` in `Via._getLayers`), so on 4-layer boards a drilled via "skipped" the inner layers it physically crosses. Anything that reads `layers` to decide per-layer interaction gets this wrong — most visibly `@tscircuit/copper-pour-solver`, whose `via.layers.includes(layer)` test makes inner-plane pours flood **solid over foreign-net via barrels** (no antipad → every through-via shorts to the inner plane in the exported gerbers; invisible to DRC because the pour is resolved after checks).
- `pcb_via.layers` now lists every copper layer between `fromLayer` and `toLayer` (inclusive, in from→to order), derived from the subcircuit layer count via a new `getViaSpanLayers` util. Both `<via>` and the imported-circuit-json `PcbVia` path use it; an explicit `layers` prop on `PcbVia` still wins, and 2-layer boards are unchanged (`[top, bottom]`).

## Testing

- Full `bun test` suite green (no existing test depends on the 2-entry shape); `bunx tsc --noEmit` clean.
- New `tests/components/primitive-components/via-layer-span.test.tsx`:
  - 4-layer `top→bottom` via → `["top", "inner1", "inner2", "bottom"]` (fails before this change: `["top", "bottom"]`)
  - 4-layer `top→inner2` via → `["top", "inner1", "inner2"]`
  - 2-layer via unchanged → `["top", "bottom"]`
- Existing via snapshot tests pass unchanged; port initialization is untouched (the span is computed at pcb-primitive render, when the board's layer count is known — before tree attachment the util falls back to `[fromLayer, toLayer]`).

This fixes schematic matchpack input generation so unconstrained two-pin components connected to power and/or ground get the expected rotation before layout.

Changes:

- Adds core-side rotation constraints for two-pin components with default available rotations.
- Places power-connected pins on y+ and ground-connected pins on y-.

title: Introduce Unified Current and Voltage Waveform Visualization for Simulation Graph Rendering
description: Expands the simulation visualization pipeline to treat current and voltage waveforms as first-class graph types, enabling combined rendering, selection, and analysis within a single simulation view.
body:
Expands the simulation visualization pipeline to treat current and voltage waveforms as first-class graph types, enabling combined rendering, selection, and analysis within a single simulation view.

This PR includes:

* Support for `simulation_transient_current_graph` rendering
* Current graph selection via `simulation_transient_current_graph_ids`
* Mixed current and voltage waveform visualization in a shared graph
* Probe-aware labeling, coloring, and legend generation
* Automatic current-only, voltage-only, and mixed-mode Y-axis labeling
* Support for current probe display options and division-based scaling
* Current probe metadata integration throughout the graph rendering pipeline
* SVG data attributes for transient current graph identification
* Schematic simulation view support for embedded current waveform displays
* Comprehensive snapshot and regression coverage for current-only and mixed waveform scenarios

This establishes a unified simulation graphing framework that enables current and voltage measurements to be visualized, compared, and analyzed together using a consistent rendering and interaction model.

title: render ammeter symbol mark as path in all directions
description: Updates the DC ammeter symbol to render the center A as SVG path primitives instead of text, ensuring consistent rendering across symbol previews and downstream schematic SVG output.
body:
This PR updates the DC ammeter symbol so the center A is rendered as SVG path primitives instead of a text primitive.

**Motivation**

dc_ammeter_horz previously defined the A using literal text. Some downstream renderers, like circuit-to-svg, only substitute special symbol text such as {REF} and {VAL}, so the literal A could be dropped or rendered inconsistently.

By making the A part of the symbol geometry, the ammeter mark renders consistently across symbol previews and downstream schematic SVG output.

**Changes**

Replaced the ammeter A text primitive with path primitives.
Kept {REF} and {VAL} as text primitives.
Updated the vertical ammeter to preserve the path-based A upright.
Updated the horizontal and vertical ammeter SVG snapshots.

title: Introduce SPICE Current Probe Instrumentation with Automatic Sense Sources and Transient Waveform Capture
description: Adds end-to-end SPICE netlist support for simulation current probes by automatically instrumenting circuits with zero-volt sense sources and exporting current waveforms during transient analysis.
body:
Adds end-to-end SPICE netlist support for simulation current probes by automatically instrumenting circuits with zero-volt sense sources and exporting current waveforms during transient analysis.

This PR includes:

* Current probe processing in SPICE netlist generation
* Automatic insertion of 0V sense voltage sources for current measurement
* Support for both port-based and net-based current probes
* Node resolution from source ports and source nets
* Automatic `.PRINT TRAN` and `.SAVE` generation for current waveforms
* Unified transient output handling for voltage and current probes
* Probe metadata emission for downstream waveform mapping
* Collision-safe sense source naming
* Support for multiple simultaneous current probes
* Comprehensive transient simulation regression coverage

This establishes the infrastructure required to measure branch current in generated SPICE simulations and enables transient current waveform visualization alongside existing voltage probe support.

title: fix: rebuild selector cache so fallback net labels include async-loaded ports
description: Fixes the issue where fallback net labels do not include ports from connectors that are loaded asynchronously, ensuring all relevant ports are represented in the labels.
body:
## Problem
When a fallback net label names every port on a net (e.g. `U1_OUT/R1_pin1`), a trace between a resistor and a `<connector standard="usb_c">` only produced `R2_pin1` — the connector's pin was missing from the label.

## Root Cause
The connector adds its ports via an async parts-engine fetch that resolves in a later render cycle. The `OptimizeSelectorCache` phase memoizes `selectAll("port")` into `_cachedSelectAllQueries` in the first cycle, before those ports exist. When the ports arrive, `_markDirty` correctly re-marks the phase dirty, but there was no `updateOptimizeSelectorCache` method — so the dispatcher's `update${phase}?.()` call silently no-op'd and the stale, connector-less cache was reused.

## Fix
Add the missing `updateOptimizeSelectorCache` lifecycle method (every phase follows the `doInitial`/`update` pattern, and the dispatcher already invokes it when a phase is dirty). It clears the memoized selector caches and re-warms them from the current tree, so `selectAll("port")` reflects async-loaded ports.

## Summary

This fixes `createComponentsFromCircuitJson` so imported `pcb_smtpad` elements with `shape: "rotated_rect"` are rendered correctly.

Previously, rotated rectangular SMT pads were skipped during import, which meant:
- no `rotated_rect` pad was created in the PCB db

This change:
- adds `rotated_rect` handling in `createComponentsFromCircuitJson`

- updates the PCB snapshot to reflect the rendered rotated pad

title: migration: migrate to renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB and widen snapshot types to Uint8Array
description: Migrates from the deprecated renderGLTFToPNGBufferFromGLBBuffer to renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB, updating snapshot types to Uint8Array to avoid unnecessary Buffer conversions.
body:
## Summary

Bumps \`poppygl\` from \`0.0.20\` to \`0.0.24\` and migrates all call sites from the deprecated \`renderGLTFToPNGBufferFromGLBBuffer\` to the new \`renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB\`. The new function returns \`Uint8Array\` instead of \`Buffer\`, so snapshot types and \`compareAndCreateDiff\` are widened accordingly — avoiding unnecessary \`Buffer.from\` conversions at call sites.

### Changes

- `cli/build/worker-output-generators.ts` — updated import and call site
- `cli/build/build-preview-images.ts` — updated import and call site
- `lib/shared/process-snapshot-file.ts` — updated import, call site, and \`png3d\` type (\`Buffer → Uint8Array\`)
- `lib/shared/compare-images.ts` — widened \`compareAndCreateDiff\` params to \`Uint8Array\`; internal \`Buffer.from\` cast before passing to \`looksSame\`
- Updated 3D snapshot baselines to match new renderer output

### Why

Using the deprecated export risks breakage on the next major \`poppygl\` bump. The new function name is consistent with the rest of the \`poppygl\` API surface (\`renderGLTFToPNGFromURL\`, \`renderGLTFToPNGBuffer\`, etc.).

## Test plan

- [x] Type check passes (\`tsc --noEmit\`)
- [x] 3D snapshot baselines updated
- [ ] \`bun test\` passes

title: Upgraded poppygl from 0.0.18 → ^0.0.24 and migrate from renderGLTFToPNGBufferFromGLBBuffer → renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB
description: Upgrades poppygl to reduce PNG output size by 55 for 3D previews, improving load times for circuit previews.
body:
## Summary

3D preview PNG renders were unnecessarily large, slowing down circuit preview load times. This PR upgrades `poppygl` from `0.0.18` to `0.0.24` and migrates to its updated API (`renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB`), resulting in a consistent ~55% reduction in PNG output size across all 3D render scenarios.

## Changes

- **Upgraded** `poppygl` from `0.0.18` → `^0.0.24`
- **Migrated** API call from `renderGLTFToPNGBufferFromGLBBuffer` → `renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB` in `lib/render3dPng.ts`
- **Updated** all 3D PNG and SVG snapshots to reflect improved rendering output

## Performance Impact

| Snapshot | Before | After | Reduction |
|---|---|---|---|
| `3d-infinite-grid-with-model` | 755 KB | 282 KB | **63%** |
| `3d-cadmodel-step` | 292 KB | 114 KB | **61%** |
| `3d-infinite-grid-test` | 279 KB | 118 KB | **58%** |
| `3d-infinite-grid` | 302 KB | 122 KB | **60%** |
| `3d-cadmodel-step-parentheses-path` | 474 KB | 248 KB | **48%** |
| `3d-png` | 66 KB | 28 KB | **58%** |

Smaller PNG payloads mean faster 3D preview loads for every circuit on svg.tscircuit.com — especially relevant for boards with complex CAD models.

title: Introduce End-to-End Current Graph Generation Pipeline for SPICE Simulation Results
description: Extends the simulation engine beyond voltage-only outputs by introducing a unified graph-processing pipeline that converts SPICE current measurements into first-class simulation graph artifacts.
body:
Extends the simulation engine beyond voltage-only outputs by introducing a unified graph-processing pipeline that converts SPICE current measurements into first-class simulation graph artifacts.

This PR includes:

* Unified simulation graph abstraction supporting both voltage and current waveforms
* Conversion of SPICE current measurements into `simulation_transient_current_graph` elements
* Current probe metadata extraction from SPICE output
* Automatic preservation of current probe identities, names, and source associations
* Shared graph interpolation and transient resampling logic for voltage and current data
* Support for mixed voltage/current simulation outputs from a single run
* Serialization of current graphs into Circuit JSON simulation artifacts
* Public simulation graph conversion APIs and exports
* Enhanced simulation graph rendering and snapshot coverage
* Regression tests covering voltage-only, current-only, and mixed waveform simulations

### Note we clean up core, move functions in their own files, and keep the index clear

This establishes a complete simulation-results pipeline where both voltage and current measurements are generated, normalized, serialized, and rendered using a shared graph infrastructure.

Additionally, it includes a new script for generating semantic reference JSON files, which will aid in the creation of system diagrams.

title: refactor(core): migrate 3D snapshot rendering to poppygl's current GLB API
description: Refactors the 3D snapshot matcher to use poppygls current GLB rendering function, replacing the older helper and simplifying the snapshot pipeline.
body:
## Summary

This PR refactors the 3D snapshot matcher in `tscircuit/core` to use `poppygl`'s current GLB rendering function.

The existing matcher used the older `renderGLTFToPNGBufferFromGLBBuffer` helper. This change migrates that path to `renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB` and keeps the PNG as a `Uint8Array` through the normal write path, only converting to `Buffer` where `looks-same` requires it.

## Changes

- replace `renderGLTFToPNGBufferFromGLBBuffer` with `renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB`
- align the snapshot render path with the current `poppygl` GLB API
- keep PNG data as `Uint8Array` until the image comparison boundary
- preserve existing snapshot matcher behavior

## Why

This keeps `tscircuit/core` aligned with the current renderer surface and simplifies the snapshot pipeline by using the newer GLB render function directly.

## Verification

- passed: `bun test ./tests/fixtures/circuit-3d-snapshot.test.tsx`

title: Introduce Ammeter component  and Simulation Workflow with Current Graph Generation and Visualization
description: Completes the ammeter feature by connecting schematic ammeters to the simulation engine and waveform visualization pipeline, enabling current measurements to flow from circuit definition through rendered simulation results.
body: Completes the ammeter feature by connecting schematic ammeters to the simulation engine and waveform visualization pipeline, enabling current measurements to flow from circuit definition through rendered simulation results. This PR includes: * Native `` component registration and JSX support * Automatic generation of simulation current probes from ammeter components * Integration of transient current graph results into simulation rendering * Current graph persistence during simulation execution * Support for rendering current waveforms alongside voltage waveforms * Probe-aware labeling, coloring, and display option propagation * Mixed current/voltage simulation graph visualization * Current waveform support in schematic simulation SVG views * Dependency upgrades required for current simulation infrastructure * Snapshot coverage for ammeter rendering and current graph visualization This establishes a complete current-measurement workflow, allowing ammeters to generate simulation probes, capture transient current data, and visualize current waveforms directly within simulation outputs.

title: Fix inferred board-surface origin for CAD meshes
description: Fixes the inferred origin alignment for CAD meshes by centering them on the footprint origin based on the board-contact patch when an explicit model origin position is not provided.
body:
## Summary

- infer `center_of_component_on_board_surface` from the mesh's board-contact patch when `model_origin_position` is not provided
- keep `center` alignment inference based on the full mesh bounding box
- pass the full mesh into `getMeshOrigin` so origin inference can inspect triangle vertices
- update unit coverage and the TO-92 repro/snapshot to verify footprint-origin alignment

## Why

Some CAD models rely on inferred origin alignment instead of an explicit `model_origin_position`. Before this change, meshes using `center_of_component_on_board_surface` could stay at their raw local origin, which offset the model from the footprint origin. This change recenters those meshes using the contact area on the board plane while preserving the expected board-surface height.

title: refactor: rename renderGLTFToPNGBufferFromGLBBuffer → renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB and extract shared renderGlbToPng test helper
description: Summary Bumps poppygl to 0.0.24, which renames renderGLTFToPNGBufferFromGLBBuffer  renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB Updates all 35 call sites to the new API Introduces testshelpers.ts with a renderGlbToPng utility that composes getBestCameraPosition  renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB Refactors 39 test files to use the helper, removing 114 lines of repeated boilerplate  Why This Matters Every snapshot and repro test previously duplicated the same 3-step pattern: ts  repeated verbatim in 39 files before this PR const cameraOptions  getBestCameraPosition(circuitJson) expect( renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB(glb as ArrayBuffer, cameraOptions), ).toMatchPngSnapshot(import.meta.path)  With the new helper: ts expect( renderGlbToPng(glb as ArrayBuffer, circuitJson), ).toMatchPngSnapshot(import.meta.path)  The helper also accepts optional render overrides and CameraFitOptions, covering tests with custom resolution, supersampling, background color, or camera presets: ts renderGlbToPng(glb, circuitJson,  width: 2048, supersampling: 2 ) renderGlbToPng(glb, circuitJson,  backgroundColor: 1, 1, 1 ,  preset: top_down, ortho: true )   Impact 55 files changed, net 114 lines Camera-position logic is now centralized  future changes to default camera behavior only need to be made in one place Adding new snapshot tests is significantly less boilerplate-heavy going forward  Test Plan   Existing snapshot tests pass with updated poppygl version   renderGlbToPng helper produces identical output to the previous inline pattern   Tests with custom camera options (spread pattern) continue to work via the extraOptions  cameraFitOptions arguments
body:
## Summary

- Bumps `poppygl` to `^0.0.24`, which renames `renderGLTFToPNGBufferFromGLBBuffer` → `renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB`
- Updates all 35+ call sites to the new API
- Introduces `tests/helpers.ts` with a `renderGlbToPng` utility that composes `getBestCameraPosition` + `renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB`
- Refactors **39 test files** to use the helper, removing ~114 lines of repeated boilerplate

## Why This Matters

Every snapshot and repro test previously duplicated the same 3-step pattern:

```ts
// repeated verbatim in 39+ files before this PR
const cameraOptions = getBestCameraPosition(circuitJson)
expect(
  renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB(glb as ArrayBuffer, cameraOptions),
).toMatchPngSnapshot(import.meta.path)
```

With the new helper:

```ts
expect(
  renderGlbToPng(glb as ArrayBuffer, circuitJson),
).toMatchPngSnapshot(import.meta.path)
```

The helper also accepts optional render overrides and `CameraFitOptions`, covering tests with custom resolution, supersampling, background color, or camera presets:

```ts
renderGlbToPng(glb, circuitJson, { width: 2048, supersampling: 2 })
renderGlbToPng(glb, circuitJson, { backgroundColor: [1, 1, 1] }, { preset: "top_down", ortho: true })
```

## Impact

- **55 files changed**, net **−114 lines**
- Camera-position logic is now centralized — future changes to default camera behavior only need to be made in one place
- Adding new snapshot tests is significantly less boilerplate-heavy going forward

## Test Plan

- [ ] Existing snapshot tests pass with updated poppygl version
- [ ] `renderGlbToPng` helper produces identical output to the previous inline pattern
- [ ] Tests with custom camera options (spread pattern) continue to work via the `extraOptions` / `cameraFitOptions` arguments

title: Migrate GLB render snapshot tests to poppygl's renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB API
description: Updates the GLB-to-PNG snapshot test path to use poppygls current renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB API and refreshes the affected visual snapshots.
body:
## Summary

This PR updates the GLB-to-PNG snapshot test path to use `poppygl`'s current `renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB` API and refreshes the affected visual snapshots.

## What changed

- upgraded `poppygl` from `^0.0.7` to `^0.0.24`
- upgraded `jscad-electronics` from `^0.0.48` to `^0.0.137`
- replaced `renderGLTFToPNGBufferFromGLBBuffer` with `renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB` in the `jscad-electronics` example tests
- refreshed the 3 affected PNG snapshots to match the current renderer output

## Why

The older test helper path is no longer the preferred `poppygl` API. This change keeps the visual regression suite aligned with the upstream renderer interface while preserving coverage for:
- GLB export validity
- embedded color attributes in generated meshes
- rendered PNG snapshot output for representative footprints

## Verification

Ran:
- `bun test tests/jscad-electronics-examples/jscad-electronics-example01.test.ts`
- `bun test tests/jscad-electronics-examples/jscad-electronics-example01-flat.test.ts`
- `bun test tests/jscad-electronics-examples/jscad-electronics-example02-flat.test.ts`

All 3 tests pass.

## Notes

This PR is intentionally scoped to test/runtime compatibility and snapshot validation for the updated renderer path.

title: Render true 3D board image for "3D" PNG exports instead of a flat SVG approximation
description: Switches the 3D export path to render an actual 3D image of the board via circuit-json-to-3d-png, ensuring the downloaded file matches user expectations for a 3D export.
body: ## Overview Downloading a circuit as a **3D PNG** from the editor previously produced a flat, SVG-based "simple 3D" approximation (`convertCircuitJsonToSimple3dSvg` rasterized through a canvas). This switches the 3D export path to render an actual 3D image of the board via `circuit-json-to-3d-png`, so the downloaded file matches what users expect from a "3D" export. ## Before ## After ## What changed - `downloadCircuitPng` now renders the `3d` format with `renderCircuitJsonTo3dPng` (perspective camera + real board textures) and saves the resulting PNG bytes directly — no SVG → canvas re-encode step. - Refactored the export function so each format produces a `Blob` through a single path (`renderCircuitToPng`), removing the previous nullable `blob`/`svg` reconciliation. The SVG formats (schematic / pcb / assembly / pinout) are unchanged in behavior. - Dropped the now-unused direct dependency `circuit-json-to-simple-3d`. ## Before / After - **Before:** "3D" download → flat shaded SVG, rasterized to PNG. - **After:** "3D" download → true 3D render of the board from a `top-left-corner` camera at 2048px board-texture resolution. ## User impact The 3D export is a user-facing tool in the editor; this makes its output genuinely 3D, which is the main value of the change. ## Notes - Output filename (`_3d.png`) and the export error/toast behavior are unchanged. - 3D rendering runs client-side; for very large boards it is heavier than the old SVG path. - `bun run typecheck` passes and Biome formatting is clean.
